findings.title: Missing HTML character escaping in JSON string encoding
findings.rationale: The diff removes the code that escapes '<', '>', and '&' characters in JSON strings (lines 1950-1953). These characters are untrusted input from user-provided strings (via AppendJSONString) and are directly written into JSON output without escaping. This allows XSS when the JSON is embedded in HTML contexts (e.g., <script> tags), as '<' and '>' can break HTML structure and inject malicious scripts.
findings.suggestion: Reintroduce the escaping of '<', '>', and '&' characters in AppendJSONString to prevent XSS when JSON is embedded in HTML.

evidence.ref: XSS via unescaped HTML characters in JSON output
